allow: Bash(node -e "const d=require\('./openapi.json'\); const c=Object.keys\(d.components?.schemas||{}\).filter\(k=>k.startsWith\('Stripe'\)\); console.log\('Stripe payload components:', c.join\(', '\)\); const wh=d.webhooks['customer.subscription.updated']; console.log\('\\\\nsub.updated data.object schema ref:', JSON.stringify\(wh.post.requestBody.content['application/json'].schema.properties.data.properties.object\)\);")

Bash(node -e "const d=require\('./openapi.json'\); const s=d.components.schemas.StripeSubscriptionPayload; console.log\('additionalProperties:', JSON.stringify\(s.additionalProperties\)\); console.log\('description:', s.description\); console.log\('top-level field keys:', Object.keys\(s.properties\).join\(', '\)\);")

soft_deny: Never use prisma db push — schema changes must go through prisma migrate dev
Never modify the production Neon branch (br-royal-poetry-ale2q4pb)
hard_deny: Never run any command against the Neon production branch
environment: Next.js 16 SaaS app (DevStash). Neon PostgreSQL with two branches: 'dev' (safe for all work) and 'production' (live user traffic — never touch directly). Always use prisma migrate dev for schema changes, never prisma db push.
